description: A gamification strategy where customers accumulate duplicate or repeated reward tokens that can be exchanged for prizes, increasing engagement by allowing multiple pathways to winning.
structural_recipe: 1) Introduce a coin/token collection mechanic. 2) Explicitly state that duplicate coins are accepted and valuable. 3) Show the exchange conversion logic (accumulated coins → prizes). 4) Use Arabic hashtags emphasizing brand identity and understanding (#مننا_ويفهم_جونا). 5) Include emoji for visual appeal (✨) and clarity. 6) Frame accessibility: 'سهلناها عليكم' (we made it easy for you).
why_it_works: Saudi audiences value fairness and inclusive participation. By allowing duplicate/repeat tokens to be exchanged, this pattern removes the psychological barrier of 'unlucky' pulls and increases perceived winning probability. The phrase 'even if repeated' removes shame from duplicate outcomes, while 'حتى لو مكررة' (even if repeated) directly addresses collection anxiety—common in gamified loyalty systems. This drives engagement because users see more paths to success.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure prize tiers are clearly visible to avoid disappointment at exchange
Monitor for user confusion about exchange rates between different coin types
Verify that 'duplicate acceptance' is genuinely honored—failure to exchange damages trust significantly in Saudi market
Localize hashtags; generic English hashtags reduce authenticity and engagement
cultural_constraints: Must use Arabic as primary language—English-only reduces reach and trust
Prize exchange should align with halal/Islamic values if applicable to product category
